steps.action: Agbadza is an Ewe music and dance that evolved f with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat.
steps.text: Agbadza is an Ewe music and dance that evolved f with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat.

steps.action: It came from a very old war dance called Atrikpu
steps.text: It came from a very old war dance called Atrikpui and usually performed by the Ewe people of the Volta Region of Ghana, particularly during the Hogbetsotso Festival, a celebration by the Anlo Ewe people.

steps.action: In addition, it is also performed by Togolese an
steps.text: In addition, it is also performed by Togolese and Beninese of Ewe descent.

steps.action: The dance has five movements in performing it, 1
steps.text: The dance has five movements in performing it, 1.

steps.action: Vutsortsor which is the main dance, 3.
steps.text: Vutsortsor which is the main dance, 3.

steps.action: Hatsatsa- historical songs are performed along w
steps.text: Hatsatsa- historical songs are performed along with Gankogui and Atoke, 5.

steps.action: Vutsortsor- finally, another round of the main d
steps.text: Vutsortsor- finally, another round of the main dance which usually last for a number of hours.

steps.action: The dance is usually played at funerals, wedding
steps.text: The dance is usually played at funerals, weddings, and parties.

steps.action: Everyone is welcome to join in the dance, unlike
steps.text: Everyone is welcome to join in the dance, unlike other Ewe dances, which sometimes are reserved for people of a certain age, religion, or gender.

steps.action: The dance is sometimes known as the “chicken dan
steps.text: The dance is sometimes known as the “chicken dance” due to the bird-like motions required for the dance.

steps.action: In order to train their warriors to be ready for
steps.text: In order to train their warriors to be ready for battle, the Ewes used various songs and dances to encourage the warriors.

steps.action: Through this a dance called Atrikpui was born.
steps.text: Through this a dance called Atrikpui was born.

steps.action: This dance later evolved to Agbadza, which is no
steps.text: This dance later evolved to Agbadza, which is no longer used for war but rather in events that are more joyful.

steps.action: Today, Agbadza is the most famous and widely pla
steps.text: Today, Agbadza is the most famous and widely played Ewe dance.

steps.action: This drum also signals the dancers when they are
steps.text: This drum also signals the dancers when they are supposed to be starting to dance, through the use of drumming language.

steps.action: Kagan: This is a support drum, which has a recur
steps.text: Kagan: This is a support drum, which has a recurring rhythm throughout the performance that interacts with the bell to create a specific melody.

steps.action: Rattle/ Axatse: This is a beaded-rattle that fol
steps.text: Rattle/ Axatse: This is a beaded-rattle that follows the rhythm of the timeline closely to complement the Agbadza song.

steps.action: Banyinyi which is a short introductory in prayer
steps.text: Banyinyi which is a short introductory in prayer to the gods and ancestors, 2.

steps.action: Adzo- which is less energetic and only the maste
steps.text: Adzo- which is less energetic and only the master is made to drum along with Gankogui and Axatse, 4.

steps.action: Gankogui is an instrument in a form of a bell wh
steps.text: Gankogui is an instrument in a form of a bell where a stick is used to play.

steps.action: Atoke is also an iron banana shaped bell and pla
steps.text: Atoke is also an iron banana shaped bell and played with a small forged iron rod.

steps.action: The Atoke can be used in place of the Gankogui t
steps.text: The Atoke can be used in place of the Gankogui they are both used for the same purpose.

steps.action: Essentially, it is played at any occasion that c
steps.text: Essentially, it is played at any occasion that called for an Ewe identity emblem, since this music is known by other ethnic groups to be uniquely Ewe.

steps.action: The Ewe people went through various times of war
steps.text: The Ewe people went through various times of war and oppression before settling down in the Volta Region of Ghana and in Southern Togo.
